Learning to Assemble Alternative Plans a.title: Blocks Assemble! Learning to Assemble with Large-Scale Structured Reinforcement Learning
Learning to Assemble Alternative Plans a.summary: Assembly of multi-part physical structures is both a valuable end product for autonomous robotics, as well as a valuable diagnostic task for open-ended training of embodied intelligent agents. We introduce a naturalistic physics-based environment with a set of connectable magnet blocks inspired by children's toy kits. The objective is to assemble blocks into a succession of target blueprints. Desp

Learning to Assemble Alternative Plans a.title: Assembly Sequence Planning for Motion Planning
Learning to Assemble Alternative Plans a.summary: This paper develops a planner to find an optimal assembly sequence to assemble several objects. The input to the planner is the mesh models of the objects, the relative poses between the objects in the assembly, and the final pose of the assembly. The output is an optimal assembly sequence, namely (1) in which order should one assemble the objects, (2) from which directions should the objects be d

Learning to Assemble Alternative Plans a.title: Subassembly to Full Assembly: Effective Assembly Sequence Planning through Graph-based Reinforcement Learning
Learning to Assemble Alternative Plans a.summary: This paper proposes an assembly sequence planning framework, named Subassembly to Assembly (S2A). The framework is designed to enable a robotic manipulator to assemble multiple parts in a prespecified structure by leveraging object manipulation actions. The primary technical challenge lies in the exponentially increasing complexity of identifying a feasible assembly sequence as the number of parts

Learning to Assemble Alternative Plans a.title: Density-based Feasibility Learning with Normalizing Flows for Introspective Robotic Assembly
Learning to Assemble Alternative Plans a.summary: Machine Learning (ML) models in Robotic Assembly Sequence Planning (RASP) need to be introspective on the predicted solutions, i.e. whether they are feasible or not, to circumvent potential efficiency degradation. Previous works need both feasible and infeasible examples during training. However, the infeasible ones are hard to collect sufficiently when re-training is required for swift adaptation

assembly sequence planning stability neu.summary: The stability and convergence of the neural networks are the fundamental characteristics in the Hopfield type networks. assembly sequence planning stability neu.title: Assembly Planning from Observations under Physical Constraints
assembly sequence planning stability neu.summary: This paper addresses the problem of copying an unknown assembly of primitives with known shape and appearance using information extracted from a single photograph by an off-the-shelf procedure for object detection and pose estimation. assembly sequence planning stability neu.title: Sampling-free obstacle gradients and reactive planning in Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF)
assembly sequence planning stability neu.summary: This work investigates the use of Neural implicit representations, specifically Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF), for geometrical queries and motion planning. We show that by adding the capacity to infer occupancy in a radius to a pre-trained NeRF, we are effectively learning an approximation to a Euclidean Signed Distance Field (ESDF). assembly sequence planning stability neu.title: Explainable Machine Learning for Public Policy: Use Cases, Gaps, and Research Directions
assembly sequence planning stability neu.summary: Explainability is highly-desired in Machine Learning (ML) systems supporting high-stakes policy decisions in areas such as health, criminal justice, education, and employment. While the field of explainable ML has expanded in recent years, much of this work has not taken real-world needs into account. program synthesis CEGIS counterexample g.title: Are There Good Mistakes? A Theoretical Analysis of CEGIS
program synthesis CEGIS counterexample g.summary: Counterexample-guided inductive synthesis CEGIS is used to synthesize programs from a candidate space of programs. The technique is guaranteed to terminate and synthesize the correct program if the space of candidate programs is finite. But the technique may or may not terminate with the correct program if the candidate space of programs is infinite. In this paper, we perform a theoretical analysi

program synthesis CEGIS counterexample g.title: Counterexample-Driven Synthesis for Probabilistic Program Sketches
program synthesis CEGIS counterexample g.summary: Probabilistic programs are key to deal with uncertainty in e.g. controller synthesis. They are typically small but intricate. Their development is complex and error prone requiring quantitative reasoning over a myriad of alternative designs. To mitigate this complexity, we adopt counterexample-guided inductive synthesis (CEGIS) to automatically synthesise finite-state probabilistic programs. Our a

program synthesis CEGIS counterexample g.title: A Theory of Formal Synthesis via Inductive Learning
program synthesis CEGIS counterexample g.summary: Formal synthesis is the process of generating a program satisfying a high-level formal specification. In recent times, effective formal synthesis methods have been proposed based on the use of inductive learning. We refer to this class of methods that learn programs from examples as formal inductive synthesis. In this paper, we present a theoretical framework for formal inductive synthesis. We dis

program synthesis CEGIS counterexample g.title: Using Program Synthesis and Inductive Logic Programming to solve Bongard Problems
program synthesis CEGIS counterexample g.summary: The ability to recognise and make analogies is often used as a measure or test of human intelligence. The ability to solve Bongard problems is an example of such a test. It has also been postulated that the ability to rapidly construct novel abstractions is critical to being able to solve analogical problems. sketch based program synthesis solar-lez.summary: This paper presents a novel free-hand sketch synthesis approach addressing explicit abstraction control in class-conditional and photo-to-sketch synthesis. Abstraction is a vital aspect of sketches, as it defines the fundamental distinction between a sketch and an image. sketch based program synthesis solar-lez.title: One-shot Face Sketch Synthesis in the Wild via Generative Diffusion Prior and Instruction Tuning
sketch based program synthesis solar-lez.summary: Face sketch synthesis is a technique aimed at converting face photos into sketches. Existing face sketch synthesis research mainly relies on training with numerous photo-sketch sample pairs from existing datasets. sketch based program synthesis solar-lez.title: Unconstrained Face Sketch Synthesis via Perception-Adaptive Network and A New Benchmark
sketch based program synthesis solar-lez.summary: Face sketch generation has attracted much attention in the field of visual computing. However, existing methods either are limited to constrained conditions or heavily rely on various preprocessing steps to deal with in-the-wild cases. sketch based program synthesis solar-lez.title: Unsupervised Sketch-to-Photo Synthesis
sketch based program synthesis solar-lez.summary: Humans can envision a realistic photo given a free-hand sketch that is not only spatially imprecise and geometrically distorted but also without colors and visual details. We study unsupervised sketch-to-photo synthesis for the first time, learning from unpaired sketch-photo data where the target photo for a sketch is unknown during training. library learning program synthesis abstr.title: Leveraging Language to Learn Program Abstractions and Search Heuristics
library learning program synthesis abstr.summary: Inductive program synthesis, or inferring programs from examples of desired behavior, offers a general paradigm for building interpretable, robust, and generalizable machine learning systems. Effective program synthesis depends on two key ingredients: a strong library of functions from which to build programs, and an efficient search strategy for finding programs that solve a given task. library learning program synthesis abstr.summary: In this paper, we introduce ChainerRL, an open-source deep reinforcement learning (DRL) library built using Python and the Chainer deep learning framework. ChainerRL implements a comprehensive set of DRL algorithms and techniques drawn from state-of-the-art research in the field.
